text.title: Gce Managed Instance Groups Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules for the gce-managed-instance-groups methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check + sourced rationale + concrete remediation hook.

rule.statement: Production MIGs MUST be regional with instances distributed across ≥3 zones.
rule.rationale: Zonal MIGs fail entirely on a single-zone outage; regional MIGs survive zone loss.

rule.statement: Rolling updates MUST set explicit `maxSurge` and `maxUnavailable` (e.g. `maxSurge=1`, `maxUnavailable=0`).
rule.rationale: Default rolling update can take down too many instances at once for capacity-sensitive workloads.

rule.statement: MIGs MUST have an auto-healing policy backed by an HTTP/HTTPS health check with an explicit `--initial-delay-sec`.
rule.rationale: Without auto-healing, unhealthy instances stay in rotation; without initial delay healthy boot-up traffic gets killed prematurely.

rule.statement: Per-instance configs MUST be used for limited canary rollouts inside a MIG, never edited ad-hoc on a running instance.
rule.rationale: Ad-hoc per-instance edits are not tracked and disappear on auto-heal.

rule.statement: Stateful or long-connection workloads MUST configure backend connection draining on the load balancer before MIG resize/update.
rule.rationale: Without draining, in-flight requests are dropped during instance replacement.

rule.statement: If the input fails ALL "Applies If" preconditions or matches ANY "Skip If" disqualifier, do not apply this methodology — route the task to the adjacent sibling instead.
rule.rationale: Even flat checklists need an explicit skip leaf in the decision tree; this rule is the universal terminal.
